entries.name: PEMCertChainLib
entries.description: Library for managing PEM certificate chains.

entries.name: SP1Verifier
entries.description: Verifier contract for SP1 proofs (v5.0.0).

entries.name: TaikoRisc0Verifier
entries.description: Gating router contract to verify batches using RISC Zero.

trustedImages.description: Taiko specific Image IDs (i.e. program digest) of Risc0 programs (block proving and aggregation program separately) trusted by this verifier gateway. Only proofs for these programs can be successfully verified. Note that proofs contains image ID data within them.

entries.name: SgxVerifier
entries.description: Verifier contract for SGX proven blocks.

entries.name: TimelockController
entries.description: A timelock with access control. The current minimum delay is 3d.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: add/remove verifiers and the selectors they are mapped to.

entries.name: SignerList
entries.description: A signer list for registering agents, similar to a Multisig.

entries.name: AutomataDcapV3Attestation
entries.description: Contract managing SGX attestation certificates.

entries.name: Taiko Token
entries.description: ERC20 contract implementing the TAIKO token. It defines a list of addresses designated as non-voting.

entries.name: RiscZeroGroth16Verifier
entries.description: Verifier contract for RISC Zero Groth16 proofs (version 3.0.0).

entries.name: EmergencyMultisig
entries.description: Modular Governance contract allowing for proposing, voting on and executing encrypted proposals (e.g. for Security Council emergency proposals).

proposalCount.description: The total count of encrypted emergency proposals created.

entries.name: EncryptionRegistry
entries.description: A registry for signers (of the Security Council) to appoint agents to operate on their behalf. These agents can also register their encryption keys for encrypted emergency proposal support.

entries.name: SP1VerifierGateway
entries.description: This contract is the router for zk proof verification. It stores the mapping between identifiers and the address of onchain verifier contracts, routing each identifier to the corresponding verifier contract.

activeVerifiers.description: Verifiers that are routed to by their selector and not frozen.

allVerifiers.description: All verifiers that were ever routed to by this gateway.

entries.name: SigVerifyLib
entries.description: Library for verifying signatures.

entries.name: MainnetInbox
entries.description: The core Layer 1 entrypoint for the Taiko rollup where L2 block batches are proposed and their corresponding state transitions are proven. It manages bonds, validates batch parameters, and acts as the state machine for the L2.

entries.name: TaikoDAOController
entries.description: Middleware contract that maintains ownership of DAO-controlled assets and contracts. Its token weight does not count towards the DAO quorum.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: can add new instances without a DCAP attestation
can update the program being verified
pause and unpause the rollup system.
can update the program being verified
can add new instances without a DCAP attestation
can update the contract address for a given name
pause/unpause, manage operator and ejector roles.

entries.name: SP1Verifier
entries.description: Verifier contract for SP1 proofs (v6.0.0).

entries.name: AutomataDcapV3Attestation
entries.description: Contract managing SGX attestation certificates.

entries.name: RiscZeroVerifierRouter
entries.description: A router proxy that routes to verifiers based on selectors. The mapping can be changed by a permissioned owner (eth:0x0b144E07A0826182B6b59788c34b32Bfa86Fb711).

verifier.description: Currently used Risc0 verifier contract, selector determined manually from calldata

entries.name: QuotaManager
entries.description: Defines withdrawal limits per token.

entries.name: TaikoSP1Verifier
entries.description: Gating router contract to verify batches using SP1.

trustedPrograms.description: Taiko specific Image IDs (i.e. program digest) of SP1 programs (block proving and aggregation program separately) trusted by this verifier gateway. Only proofs for these programs can be successfully verified. Note that proofs contains image ID data within them.

entries.name: OptimisticTokenVotingPlugin
entries.description: An optimistic governance module. Proposals pass and can be executed unless 10% of votable TAIKO veto them within 7d.

minVetoRatio.description: this value /1000000 is the threshold ratio (relative to voting supply) to veto a proposal.

entries.name: MainnetERC20Vault
entries.description: Shared vault for Taiko chains for bridged ERC20 tokens.

entries.name: MainnetVerifier
entries.description: Enforces the Taiko Multiprover policy and routes to the downstream router contracts.

receivedPermissions.description: manage the prover whitelist.
manage the ejecter role

entries.name: DAO
entries.description: The main contract and entrypoint of the Aragon-based DAO governance framework. Fine-grained DAO permissions, proposals, voting and thresholds are configured here.

receivedPermissions.description: update the trusted image ids
can add new instances without a DCAP attestation
can update the program being verified
pause and unpause the rollup system.
can update the program being verified
manage trusted program hashes.
can add new instances without a DCAP attestation
manage the prover whitelist.
can update the contract address for a given name
pause/unpause, manage operator and ejector roles.

entries.name: SignalServiceForkRouter
entries.description: Routes to the Shasta SignalService post shasta fork.

entries.name: RiscZeroVerifierEmergencyStop
entries.description: A verifier wrapper for the eth:0x2a098988600d87650Fb061FfAff08B97149Fa84D that allows pausing (emergency stop) the verifier by its owner.

paused.description: verifier is stopped, cannot be used to verify new proofs or verify the integrity of receipts.

entries.name: SgxVerifier
entries.description: Verifier contract for SGX proven blocks.

entries.name: SignalService
entries.description: Facilitates secure cross-chain message passing by storing signals (messages) and state root checkpoints. It allows applications to prove that a specific L2 signal or state transition occurred via Merkle proofs.

receivedPermissions.description: affect the liveness and safety of the gateway - can transfer ownership, add and freeze verifier routes.

entries.name: MainnetBridge
entries.description: Shared bridge for Taiko chains for bridged ETH.

entries.name: Multisig
entries.description: Modular Governance contract allowing for proposing, voting on and executing proposals (e.g. for Security Council standard proposals).

entries.name: ProverWhitelist
entries.description: Defines the whitelist of addresses allowed to prove proposals. Non-whitelisted provers must wait for the permissionless proving delay before they can submit proofs.

entries.name: L1SharedAddressManager
entries.description: Maps contract names to contract addresses. Changes in this mapping effectively act as contract upgrades.

receivedPermissions.description: cancel queued transactions.
execute transactions that are ready.
manage all access control roles.
propose transactions.
add/remove verifiers and the selectors they are mapped to.
pause the verifier.

entries.name: TaikoDAOController
entries.description: Middleware contract that maintains ownership of DAO-controlled assets and contracts. Its token weight does not count towards the DAO quorum.

entries.name: PreconfWhitelist
entries.description: Contains the whitelist of addresses allowed to propose batches on L1 and issue preconfirmations. It dynamically selects a single operator for a given epoch using the Ethereum beacon block root as a source of randomness.

receivedPermissions.description: pause and unpause proofs and verification.

entries.name: SignalServiceForkRouter
entries.description: Routes to the Shasta SignalService post shasta fork.

entries.name: L2AddressManager
entries.description: Maps contract names to contract addresses. Changes in this mapping effectively act as contract upgrades.

namedAddresses.description: Don't necessarily trust because inconsistencies have been found, e.g. signal service

entries.name: AnchorForkRouter
entries.description: Routes to the Shasta Anchor contract post shasta fork.

entries.name: SignalService
entries.description: Facilitates secure cross-chain message passing by storing signals (messages) and state root checkpoints. It allows applications to prove that a specific L2 signal or state transition occurred via Merkle proofs.

entries.name: Anchor
entries.description: Immutable L2 contract that anchors L1 block details to L2 for cross-layer communication and manages EIP-1559 gas pricing for L2 operations.

entries.name: DelegateController
entries.description: Middleware contract that maintains ownership of DAO-controlled assets and contracts. Its token weight does not count towards the DAO quorum.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: can update the contract address for a given name
